free_text: Systematics of Na-24 and Na-22 Production with 400 MeV
Protons
The Carnegie Institute of Technology
440 MeV Synchrocyclotron.
The Ttargets which were formed by impregnating
'specpure' boric acid with inorganic salts were
assured of uniformity by mixing with a high-speed ball
vibrator. The pellets obtained after moulding were
uniform in thickness to within +-0.2 mil for an average
thickness of 30 mil (approx. 60-80 mg/cm**2).
the KBr target was moulded in the press but without
the inert boric acid filler.
The targets consisted of a stack of the various foils
or pellets clamped in a removable probe head.
The leading and side edges of the metallic foils were
milled after being mounted in the target holders to
assure proper alignment. The pellets which could not be
milled were carefully aligned in a special jig before
mounting.
The Cascade-Evaporation Model.
Standard gas flow, end-window beta proportional
counters. The majority of Na-22 samples had low
counting rates and were thus counted on a sharp
laboratories low-beta counter which had a background of
less than 0.5 counts/min.
In addition to beta activity measurements the
2.76 MeV gamma of Na-24 and the 1.28 MeV gamma of Na-22
were counted with a scintillation spectrometer.
The Duration of irradiation is unknown.
From consideration of magnetic field stability and
target positioning.
The activity of the samples was measured
relative to that of the monitors and only uncertainties
due to counting statistics need be considered.
All the pellet targets were corrected for impurities
contaminations.
The corrections amounted to 20-25% for the Sr and Y
targets, 5-10% for the Ga and Se targets, and less than
1% for the others.
Tbl.2 from Inorg.Nucl.Chem.,29(1967)2863
- By Compiler - Ne-24, which is progenitor of Na-24,
has half-Life 3.4 min. Probably, Ne-24 has decayed
before chemical proccessing.
Mg-22, which is progenitor of Na-22 has half-life
3.8 sec. Probably, Mg-22 has decayed before chemical
proccessing.
Therefore all cross sections, besides Na-24 production
from Na-23, are cumulative ones.
Corrections for New Rules
Last checking has been done.
